critic.verdict: An exceptionally fluent and well-synced chart that tracks song energy perfectly, though its intense eighth-note-to-jump transitions push the difficulty slightly too high for a standard Easy rating.

issues.problem: An eighth-note stream of 5 notes directly ending on a JUMP (LD) is a highly advanced technique that exceeds the expected physical coordination of a standard Easy chart.

Releasing a hold on one foot at the exact same millisecond as tapping a new note on another foot (e.g., D release to L tap) occurs frequently throughout the chart and can be disorienting for beginners.
The eighth-note run into a JUMP LR at the end of the chorus introduces a sudden spike in physical difficulty right before the final hold.

critic.verdict: Highly repetitive and copy-pasted stream patterns that ignore physical flow, creating awkward continuous spins/crossovers inappropriate for a Medium chart.

issues.problem: Repeating L-R-U-D pattern for 17 seconds creates continuous 8th-note spins/crossovers, which is extremely exhausting and inappropriate for a Medium difficulty chart.
Extreme repetition of the exact same 'Jump LR -> U -> D -> L' sequence for nearly 40 seconds, making the gameplay feel very lazy and monotonous.
Repetitive 'D-U-L-R' stream doesn't follow any melodic variation and feels heavily copy-pasted.

critic.verdict: An incredibly monotonous and repetitive chart filled with awkward triple-panel jumps (hands) and severe panel bias.

issues.problem: Extreme repetition of the JUMP LR -> tap U eighth-note loop. This creates an exhausting, highly monotonous pattern that completely ignores the actual rhythm and variation of the music.
Simultaneous JUMP LR and HOLD D events require hitting three panels at once (Left, Down, Right), forcing awkward bracket stomps or 'hands' without proper progression or telegraphing.

Severe panel imbalance. Left and Right are utilized almost exclusively for jumps, while Up is used for nearly all single taps, leaving Down practically unused. This neglects natural foot-flow and rotation.
